data.title: Lockheed Martin appoints Evan Scott as CFO
data.description:
• Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) announced on Thursday the appointment of Evan Scott as the company's senior vice president and chief financial officer (CFO) succeeding Jesus "Jay" Malave, who advised the company that he is pursuing other opportunities.
  • Scott, a 26-year Lockheed Martin finance executive, has served as the company's treasurer and the CFO of two business areas, said the company, adding that "the appointment is effective immediately."
  • The company will hold its first quarter 2025 earnings webcast as scheduled on Tuesday, April 22, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: DocuSign's Momentum event sees positive views from analysts
data.description:
Analysts were largely positive after attending DocuSign's (NASDAQ:DOCU) user conference Momentum held on Wednesday. 

Shares of DocuSign were largely flat premarket on Thursday.

Evercore maintained an In Line rating and $90 price target on the stock.

Evercore Analysts led by Kirk Materne said they continue to believe that  DocuSign is making solid steps towards being recognized as a 'platform' with capabilities well beyond the core e-signature, and the 10,000 IAM customer data point validates the customer appetite for a true Agreement 'platform.'

"While the macro will likely remain an overhang in the near-term, DOCU is emerging as an interesting idea heading into the 2H and into CY26, especially if IAM momentum can continue to drive strong billings/NRR [Net revenue retention] improvement," the analysts added.

Intelligent Agreement Management, or IAM, is a cloud software repository to centrally store, manage, and analyze agreements across an organization, unlocking data trapped in agreements to drive efficiencies, uncover opportunities to lower costs, and reduce risk, according to the company.

"Overall, we come away from the event feeling incrementally more positive than DocuSign’s platform story is starting to resonate in a more meaningful way with customers as DOCU continues to pioneer the analytics and insights aspect of company agreements," said Materne and his team.

Meanwhile, Needham kept its Hold rating on DocuSign.

Needham Analyst Scott Berg and his team said they attended Docusign Momentum and are impressed how quickly Docusign is pivoting to the new IAM solution, even as the existing customer base remains e-signature orientated.

IAM now has 10,000 customers. While concentrated in the small and medium-sized business, or SMB, space, this is impressive given the recency of the launch, the higher price point, and the initial focus on prerenewal expansion, the analysts added.

The analysts expect additional ramp-up as Docusign shifts SMBs to a self-service option to free up capacity for complex company-wide IAM sales.

Innovation at the conference was focused on showing the IAM platform, including a new AI agent, a centralized workflow management solution, a post-signing obligation management solution, and a significant embrace of customer experience and third-party functionality, the analysts noted.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures mixed after tech led selloff
data.description:
Stock index futures were mixed on Thursday, while yields gained, as Wall Street hoped for a rebound a day after a tech-driven selloff.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.4%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.8%, and Dow futures (INDU) -1.5%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.29%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.80%.

Wall Street on Wednesday ended deep in the red after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell expressed concerns about the likely higher-than-anticipated effects from tariffs, including possibly putting pressure on the central bank's dual mandate of stable prices and strong employment.

"Ahead of the Easter weekend, risk assets sold off again, as ongoing trade tensions and hawkish comments from Fed Chair Powell led to growing concern about the near-term outlook, particularly if the Fed would be reluctant to ease policy," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"In terms of the latest on the trade front, investors were most concerned about chipmakers yesterday, which followed the new restrictions from the Donald Trump administration on semiconductor exports to China," Allen said.

On the economic front, the initial jobless claims unexpectedly dropped in the past week. 

Housing starts also dropped more than expected in March, and building permits surprised higher.

Markets will be closed tomorrow on account of Good Friday.

data.title: Texas jury orders Musk's X to pay $105M to VidStream in tech patent case
data.description:
A Texas jury on Wednesday found Elon Musk's social media platform X guilty of infringing VidStream LLC's technology and has ordered the company to pay $105M.

VidStream said that X, along with now defunct Vine and Periscope, violated its technologies for creating video content and had initially sought to get over $600M in damages.

VidStream filed a case against the company, when it was still known as Twitter, for IP infringement in 2016. They alleged that the social media platform's video services violated technologies that allowed for "rapidly creating and distributing user-generated video content over a variety of networks."

According to the lawsuit, the technology patents let users make video content for distribution on social media platforms, webpages, and television broadcasts.

X tried to defend itself, saying its technology was different from VidStream's and that the patents were invalid.

The case is VidStream LLC v. Twitter Inc., Civil Action No. 3:16-cv-00764, filed in the U.S. District Court, Northern District of Texas, Dallas Division.

More on X

  • Musk's X could be fined $1B+ for breaching EU's DSA law - NYT
  • Elon Musk says xAI has acquired X in all-stock deal
  • X adds more companies to lawsuit over advertising boycott: report
  • Banks prepare to sell billions of dollars of X loans: report
  • Elon Musk's X partners with Visa in financial services push

data.title: Charles Schwab stock gains as Q1 earnings beat, core net new assets climb
data.description:
Charles Schwab (NYSE:SCHW) stock gained 3.7% in Thursday premarket trading after the firm's Q1 core net new assets climbed 44% Y/Y amid volatile markets.

Q1 adjusted EPS of $1.04, topping the consensus estimate of $1.01, rose from $1.01 in Q4 2024 and $0.74 in last year’s Q1.

Net revenue of $5.60B, beating the $5.54B consensus, increased from $5.33B in the prior quarter and $4.74B a year ago.

Net interest revenue of $2.71B, exceeding the Visible Alpha consensus of $2.64B, grew from $2.53B in Q4 and $2.23B in Q1 2024.

Asset management and administration fees were $1.53B vs. $1.51B in the previous quarter and $1.35B a year ago.

Average bank deposit account balances of $84.3B in March increased from $83.1B in February and declined from $85.4B in December.

For Q1, core net new assets were $137.7B, an annualized growth rate of 5.5%. "This 44% year-over-year increase in asset gathering was powered by our unwavering focus on serving the needs of clients across Retail, Advisor Services, and Workplace Financial Services," said President and CEO Rick Wurster. Core net new assets of $59.1B in March 2025 rose 23% M/M and 31% Y/Y.

Client transactional sweep cash balances ended the quarter at $407.8B, a Q/Q drop of $10.8B, reflecting typical Q1 seasonality.

The firm's bank supplemental funding declined $11.8B during the quarter to $38.1B at March month-end.

Q1 average money market fund client assets of $621.5B vs. $581.0B in the prior quarter and $499.9B a year ago.

Total expenses, excluding interest, were $3.14B, vs. $3.02B in the previous quarter and $2.94B a year ago.

Earlier, Charles Schwab Non-GAAP EPS of $1.04 beats by $0.03, revenue of $5.6B beats by $60M

data.title: Enovix appoints Ryan Benton as chief financial officer
data.description:
• Enovix (NASDAQ:ENVX) has appointed Ryan Benton as chief financial officer, the company said on Thursday.
  • Benton brings over three decades of financial leadership experience. He previously held key roles at ASM International and served as CFO for multiple public companies including Silvaco and Exar Corporation.
  • Source: Press Release
